title: echinops viscosus subsp. macrolepis
intro: Echinops viscosus subsp. macrolepis is a lesser-known plant species, belonging to the genus Echinops, commonly known as globe thistle. Traditionally, this plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to various health benefits like reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. Echinops viscosus subsp. macrolepis can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and sometimes used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions and other ailments.
